label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out why small pieces of space junk can threaten satellites based on the information given. The passage mentions that even bits of space junk as small as dots of paint pose a threat. Then it explains that this is because the debris orbits our planet at speeds up to 28,200 kilometers per hour. The high speed is highlighted as the reason why these tiny paint dots have damaged the windshields of space shuttles to the point they needed to be replaced. The high speed would mean that even a small object has a lot of kinetic energy, which can cause significant damage upon impact. Looking at the answer choices: A) They travel at a very high speed. This matches the explanation given in the text. The speed is the key factor here. B) They are difficult to track by satellites. The passage doesn't mention tracking being a problem. It's more about the impact damage from speed. C) They can blow up into millions of pieces. The text doesn't say the small pieces explode. Instead, it's the existing debris that is a problem.
